scope.summary: Rust block_on reporting now follows tokio Runtime/Builder result expressions through Result::map_err before unwrap/expect/? exposes the runtime receiver.

capability_alignment.capability: success-channel-preserving Result adapter provenance for future-drive scheduling attribution
not_a_feature_list: No exact admission path was opened for Rust block_on or async/sync equivalence.
No new public semantic-pack API was added.
The verifier reuses the existing runtime-boundary-model and future-drive obligations.
Only map_err applied to an already proven tokio runtime Result is accepted; selector-only map_err or wrapper-returned Results stay closed.
The error-conversion callback remains an exception/effect boundary when the surrounding unit is oracle-excluded.
reused_capabilities: shared future-drive scheduling and future-settled value-channel obligations

new_reporting_surfaces.guard: Runtime::new must be qualified or imported from tokio::runtime, unshadowed, and map_err must adapt that proven runtime Result before source-backed ? exposes the success value.
Builder construction must already be accepted by the existing builder-chain guard; map_err may only wrap the proven build Result.
unwrap/expect consume the map_err-adapted Result only when the adapter's receiver is a proven runtime Result.

corpus_pricing.note: The third corpus occurrence initializes a struct field; non-self field lifecycle proof remains closed.

assertions: Runtime::new().map_err(convert)? local bindings report future-drive and future-settled obligations.
Builder::new_current_thread().enable_all().build().map_err(convert)? local bindings report the same obligations.
Runtime::new().unwrap().map_err(convert)? stays closed because map_err is no longer attached to a proven runtime Result success channel.
A Rust Runtime::new().map_err(convert)? fixture appears under oracle_exclusions.by_obligation for future-drive scheduling.
The same unit keeps exact admission closed and remains reporting-only.

current_crates_gate.note: The local crates surface has no proof-backed Rust map_err block_on bridge rows; the gate is used for hard soundness and runtime comparison.

summary.primary_reason_note: The unit also carries async-await and async-block obligations, which remain the primary scheduling subreason by rule order.

closed_surfaces.reason: Wrapper calls can change the success value before map_err observes the Result.
At that point map_err is an arbitrary method on a runtime value, not a Result success-channel adapter.

closed_surfaces.surface: struct field initialization such as runtime: Runtime::new().map_err(...)? followed by plugin.runtime.block_on(...)
closed_surfaces.reason: Constructor-assigned field lifecycle and non-self field identity proof remain separate capabilities.

closed_surfaces.surface: Exact convergence between block_on, await, and synchronous payload values
closed_surfaces.reason: Future drive scheduling, wake/poll behavior, cancellation/liveness, result-channel, and complete receiver/type identity contracts remain missing evidence.

next_followups: Price and model constructor-assigned runtime fields only after field lifecycle, mutation, and non-self receiver identity proof exist.
Consider additional success-channel-preserving Result adapters only one at a time with hard negatives.
Keep exact block_on/await convergence closed until scheduling and result-channel contracts become proof-backed.
